# LoomLance

> The developer-first freelance business platform. LoomLance brings the whole business side of freelancing — clients, projects, contracts, invoicing, time tracking, expenses, reports — into one place, with native GitHub integration so developers stay in their workflow. Solo plan is free forever; paid plans (Freelancer $19/mo, Studio $49/mo) add time tracking, branding, recurring invoices, and more, each with a 14-day free trial.

## Key pages
- [Home](https://loomlance.com/): Product overview — what LoomLance does and who it's for (freelance developers and small dev studios).
- [Pricing](https://loomlance.com/pricing): Plans and prices (Solo free, Freelancer, Studio, Agency coming soon) with a feature comparison and FAQ.
- [Blog](https://loomlance.com/blog): Feature releases, product updates, and press from the LoomLance team.
- [Contact](https://loomlance.com/contact): Get in touch with the team.

## What makes LoomLance different
- GitHub integration: connect repos to projects, pull issues into a kanban, smart-commits reference task keys so shipped work links to billed work.
- Scope-creep change requests: send a client an approvable link for added cost/time; approval flows straight into an invoice.
- Credential vault: store API keys, .env secrets, and client logins encrypted, organized by project.
- Online payments: clients pay invoices by card (Stripe) or PayPal; funds go directly to the freelancer.

## Facts
- Category: Freelance business management / invoicing software for software developers.
- Free plan: Solo — 1 active project, unlimited clients, contracts, invoicing, and payments; no credit card required.
- Paid plans: Freelancer ($19/mo or $190/yr) and Studio ($49/mo or $490/yr), 14-day free trial, cancel anytime.
- Featured in KrispiTech: "The Developer-First Freelance Platform: Why LoomLance Is Built Differently."
